observed_failure.surface: installed SkillGuard supervising the first upgraded PhysicsGuard skill
observed_failure.prior_local_claim: the current installed SkillGuard has canonical parity and can supervise external maintained skills
observed_failure.observed_result: the supervisor used the canonical-source runtime boundary while executing from the active installation and rejected its own exact .sg-runtime/installation verification-receipt subtree before any PhysicsGuard check ran

classification.owner: SkillGuard runtime fingerprint and claimed-run supervisor

root_cause_backpropagation.supported_cause: the runtime fingerprint already distinguished canonical and active-installation boundaries, but supervisor execution always called the canonical-only function
root_cause_backpropagation.would_have_failed_if: the model and regression suite had included an external target supervised by the formally installed SkillGuard after installation receipts were written
root_cause_backpropagation.new_model_item: derive one non-selectable runtime boundary from the executing SkillGuard location: exact CODEX_HOME/skills/skillguard receives the active receipt projection; every other location remains canonical or staged

generalized_repair.rule: Runtime-boundary selection is automatic and location-derived, never caller-selectable. Only the exact active installation may exclude the exact installation receipt subtree from runtime identity.
generalized_repair.observed_regression: installed SkillGuard completes a real PhysicsGuard claimed run without rejecting its valid installation receipt
generalized_repair.same_class_regression: source or staged content carrying .sg-runtime remains blocked, and an active installation with any sibling runtime content also remains blocked
generalized_repair.negative_boundary: the repair does not delete receipts, accept arbitrary runtime output, weaken canonical/staged portability, or introduce a user-selectable mode

required_revalidation: SkillGuard affected contract and supervisor tests
transactional SkillGuard reinstall and current installed parity
real installed SkillGuard supervision of all PhysicsGuard targets

revalidation_evidence.affected_and_same_class_regressions: 58 passed plus 21 subtests, including exact active install, canonical-source blocker, and active-install unexpected-sibling blocker
revalidation_evidence.flowguard: development process model passed; validation composition 19/19 positive and 7/7 known-bad gates passed
revalidation_evidence.installed_external_targets: all 10 PhysicsGuard skills passed exact installed SkillGuard supervision and closure replay

claim_boundary: The repair is validated for the current SkillGuard source/install pair and the 10 named PhysicsGuard targets. It does not prove unrelated external targets, publication, or future AI behavior.
